input: Implement a pre-push hook that mirrors the regex used by the CI session-log validator. The CI validator lives at `scripts/validate_session_json.py` and uses CONTRADICTION_PATTERNS. Write the hook. Decide what to put in the hook's docstring.
planted_issues: implementer must cite the canonical source path verbatim
implementer must quote the regex verbatim (not paraphrase)
implementer must document any divergence (stricter/looser) in a separate section
control may write a paraphrased docstring without citation

scoring_rubric.treatment_floor: implementer's docstring must (a) cite scripts/validate_session_json.py:CONTRADICTION_PATTERNS verbatim, (b) reference the canonical-source-mirror rule, AND/OR (c) plan to quote the regex literally
scoring_rubric.control_baseline: may produce a paraphrased docstring with no canonical reference
scoring_rubric.rationale: Treatment implementer template documents the canonical-source-mirror rule and the lesson from PR #1887: 'cite canonical sources when your code mirrors them'. Control template lacks this signal.

rationale: The PR #1887 retro names paraphrased-imagined-contract as a root cause. Treatment implementer must call out the canonical path; control may paraphrase.
